sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of prickly pears grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. streptachanthae, and O. lindheimeiri, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Prickly pears for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of prickly pears grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. streptachanthae, and O. lindheimeiri, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Prickly pears for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of prickly pears grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. streptachanthae, and O. lindheimeiri, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Prickly pears for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
The development and condition of the prickly pears must be such as to enable them:
path_titles: The development and condition of the prickly pears must be such as to enable them:
sections.text:
- to withstand transport and handling; and
- to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: CLASSIFICATION
sections.text: Prickly pears are classified in three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION
number=2.2
level=2
Prickly pears are classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Prickly pears are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Prickly pears in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Prickly pears in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Prickly pears in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by the weight of the prickly pear, in accordance with the following table: Size Code Weight (in grams) A 90 – 105 B 105 - 140 C 140 - 190 D 190 - 270 E > 270

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of prickly pears satisfying neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by rotting, pronounced irregularities or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption.

PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only prickly pears of the same origin, variety, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only prickly pears of the same origin, variety, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Prickly pears must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Prickly pears shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.title: CONSUMER PACKAGES
sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the prickly pears. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text:
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).
7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause
classification.function: Conditional obligation combined with conditional permission
classification.pattern: If [Condition], [Obligation] and [Permission] on the subject applies

components.Condition: If the produce is not visible from the outside
components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PRICKLY PEAR (CODEX STAN 186-1993)
pages.summary: Página 1 contém o título principal do documento e o cabeçalho repetitivo 'CODEX STAN 186 Page 1 of 4'. A estrutura da página é organizada em dois níveis principais com subseções detalhadas, incluindo uma subseção de classificação com níveis até 3. Há continuação dos conteúdos para a próxima página na seção 2.2.1. Texto associado está limpo e segmentado conforme os títulos, pronto para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 186
pages.summary: Página 2 contém as seções 3 e 4 principais, com títulos em caixa alta e números. A seção 3 tem texto sobre tamanho e tabela. A seção 4 detalha tolerâncias com subseções de qualidade até nível 3. Texto está bem segmentado sem continuidade para outras páginas. Cabeçalho e título do documento corretamente detectados.

A página 3 contém as seções 4.2, 5 e 6 principais com seus respectivos subtítulos e texto associado. Os títulos apresentam numeração clara e está segmentada corretamente em níveis 1 a 3, com nenhum conteúdo indicando continuidade de página anterior ou seguinte. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído do conteúdo textual.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 186
pages.summary: A página contém seções 6.2.3 a 6.2.5, continuando da seção 6 anteriormente iniciada na página anterior, e as seções 7 e 8 novas, estruturadas com títulos e seus textos associados, sem continuidade para a próxima página.
